correction.reason: Initial trace and product documentation used 2026-05-08 paths and continued checkpoint numbering from the previous slice. The current slice started on 2026-05-09 and must use a single slice-level trace plus JSON companion.

behaviour: find user by Cloudflare Access provider subject
if no identity exists, find existing bootstrapped user by email
link Cloudflare Access identity to existing user
if neither identity nor email exists, create a new pending user and link the identity
resolver.purpose: Prevent first sign-in failure when the admin user has been seeded by email before their first Cloudflare Access login.

deterministic_id_strategy.review_issue: The initial membership and assignment IDs used the team suffix only, so a different admin email bootstrapped into the same team could hit primary-key conflicts before the unique user/team or role-assignment conflict handlers applied.
deterministic_id_strategy.fix: Membership and assignment IDs now include team material, user/email material and a deterministic SHA-256 digest of teamId:email.
deterministic_id_strategy.outcome: Reruns for the same user and team stay idempotent, while different admin emails in the same team receive different primary IDs.

validation.review_fix_coverage: Tests now assert that the generator uses stableDigest, teamSuffix, userSuffix and principalSuffix for deterministic IDs.

next_steps_after_merge: Run Bootstrap D1 Auth Runtime from GitHub Actions with the intended first-admin email and team values.
Configure Cloudflare Access runtime values and Worker secrets.
Sign in through Cloudflare Access as the bootstrapped user.
Verify /api/me and /api/me/permissions return the expected active team, roles and permissions.
